title: AI Dungeon maker Latitude unveils Voyage, a platform for creating AI-powered RPGs
description: Latitude's new AI-native platform, Voyage, aims to help gamers create their very own role-playing game.

title: Bond, a new social media platform, wants to use AI to help you kick your doomscrolling habit
description: Bond wants you to get off the couch and get back into the real world, its creator says. The new platform's AI system is designed to motivate users to do things away from the app.

title: YouTube expands its AI likeness detection technology to celebrities
description: YouTube is expanding its AI likeness detection tool to celebrities, giving talent and their reps a way to find and remove deepfakes.

title: CATL's new LFP battery can charge from 10 to 98% in less than 7 minutes
description: The self-heating Shenxing battery still performs even in Arctic temperatures.

title: AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 Dual Edition review: Tons of cache for tons of dollars
description: There are some practical benefits to this $899 chip, but not many.

title: What's the deal with spacesuits for the Moon? Will they be ready in time?
description: NASA is down to a single provider for a critical link in its lunar architecture.

title: Loneliness in older adults can often lead to memory impairment
description: A longitudinal study finds links to lapses in immediate and delayed recall.

title: GRAI believes AI can make music more social, not replace artists
description: AI music startup GRAI says fans want to remix tracks, not generate songs from scratch.

title: Contrary to popular superstition, AES 128 is just fine in a post-quantum world
description: A stubborn misconception is hampering the already hard work of quantum readiness.

title: The Download: turning down human noise, and LA’s stunning subway upgrade
description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The noise we make is hurting animals. title: This Scammer Used an AI-Generated MAGA Girl to Grift ‘Super Dumb’ Men
description: A med student says he’s made thousands of dollars selling photos and videos of a young conservative woman he created using generative tools. He’s not alone.

title: Digging for clues about the North Pole’s past
description: In the past, even with an icebreaker and during peak melt season, getting to the North Pole wasn’t a sure bet. title: Anthropic takes $5B from Amazon and pledges $100B in cloud spending in return
description: Amazon has made another circular AI deal: It's investing another $5 billion in Anthropic. Anthropic has agreed to spend $100 billion on AWS in return.

title: A Humanoid Robot Set a Half-Marathon Record in China
description: An autonomous robot from the company Honor ran a half marathon in 50:26, beating the human record by 7 minutes.

title: Prego Has a Dinner-Conversation-Recording Device, Capisce?
description: The pasta-sauce company has partnered with the nonprofit StoryCorps on a device designed to record family conversations around the table and save them for all time.

title: The Download: murderous ‘mirror’ bacteria, and Chinese workers fighting AI doubles
description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. title: Tech CEOs Think AI Will Let Them Be Everywhere at Once
description: Mark Zuckerberg and Jack Dorsey have different visions for how to use AI for management purposes, but both imagine a system of heightened control.

title: Colossal Biosciences said it cloned red wolves. Is it for real?
description: If you want to capture something wolflike, it’s best to embark before dawn. So on a morning this January, with the eastern horizon still pink-hued, I drove with two young scientists into a blanket of fog. title: Chinese tech workers are starting to train their AI doubles—and pushing back
description: Tech workers in China are being instructed by their bosses to train AI agents to replace them—and it’s prompting a wave of soul-searching among otherwise enthusiastic early adopters.  Earlier this month a GitHub project called Colleague Skill, which claimed workers could use it to “distill” the

title: It Takes 2 Minutes to Hack the EU’s New Age-Verification App
description: Plus: Major data breaches at a gym chain and hotel giant, a disruptive DDoS attack against Bluesky, dubious ICE hires, and more.

title: Understanding Convolutions on Graphs
description: Understanding the building blocks and design choices of graph neural networks.

title: A Gentle Introduction to Graph Neural Networks
description: What components are needed for building learning algorithms that leverage the structure and properties of graphs?

title: Distill Hiatus
description: After five years, Distill will be taking a break.

title: Adversarial Reprogramming of Neural Cellular Automata
description: Reprogramming Neural CA to exhibit novel behaviour, using adversarial attacks.

title: Weight Banding
description: Weights in the final layer of common visual models appear as horizontal bands. We investigate how and why.
